meaning: to think that somebody/something is important
examples: value somebody/something, He has come to value her advice and support.

meaning: to be different from each other in size, shape, etc.
examples: New techniques were introduced with varying degrees of success.

meaning: to think about somebody/something in a particular way
examples: view somebody/something + adv./prep., How do you view your position within the company?

meaning: to go to see a person or a place for a period of time
examples: My parents are coming to visit me next week.

meaning: to offer to do something without being forced to do it or without getting paid for it
examples: He has been volunteering for 11 years now.

meaning: to show formally by marking a paper, raising your hand, using a voting machine, etc. which person you want to win an election, or which plan or idea you support
examples: How did you vote at the last election?

meaning: to make something/somebody warm or warmer; to become warm or warmer
examples: warm something/somebody/yourself, Come in and warm yourself by the fire.

meaning: to tell somebody about something, especially something dangerous or unpleasant that is likely to happen, so that they can avoid it
examples: warn somebody, I tried to warn him, but he wouldn't listen.

meaning: to use more of something than is necessary or useful
examples: waste something, Stop wasting time and just get on with it!

meaning: to move your hand or arm from side to side in the air in order to attract attention, say hello, etc.
examples: The people on the bus waved and we waved back.

meaning: to have something on your body as a piece of clothing, a decoration, etc.
examples: He was wearing a new suit.

meaning: to have a particular weight
examples: How much do you weigh (= how heavy are you)?

meaning: to speak very quietly to somebody so that other people cannot hear what you are saying
examples: Don't you know it's rude to whisper?

meaning: used for talking about or predicting the future
examples: You'll be in time if you hurry.

meaning: to want something to happen or to be true even though it is unlikely or impossible
examples: wish (that)…, I wish I were taller.

meaning: to see something happen (typically a crime or an accident)
examples: to witness an accident/a murder/an attack

meaning: to think about something and try to decide what is true, what will happen, what you should do, etc.
examples: ‘Why do you want to know?’ ‘No particular reason. I was just wondering.’

meaning: to keep thinking about unpleasant things that might happen or about problems that you have
examples: Don't worry. We have plenty of time.

meaning: used in polite offers or invitations
examples: Would you like a sandwich?

meaning: to injure part of the body, especially by making a hole in the skin using a weapon
examples: He had been wounded in the arm.

meaning: to cover something completely in paper or other material, for example when you are giving it as a present
examples: wrap something up, He spent the evening wrapping up the Christmas presents.
